programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: The fifteenth season of the program, thanks to which we have the opportunity to observe how complicated the production processes of the simplest items we use every day can be.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.sub-title: The last secrets of the Cassini probe
programme.desc: The creators of the program present various types of phenomena and how celestial bodies function.Scientists will unravel the secrets of astronomy.The series will use innovative computer animation techniques.

programme.title: Building Germany
programme.desc: Viewers will look at the everyday life of people who work on the largest infrastructure projects in Germany.We will find out what people doing difficult and dangerous jobs do.

programme.title: The Dino Hunters
programme.desc: For years, American cowboys lived only by raising cattle.For some time now, many of them have been choosing a different business path - searching for dinosaur bones.They charge handsomely for their finds.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.sub-title: The last secrets of the Cassini probe
programme.desc: The creators of the program present various types of phenomena and how celestial bodies function.Scientists will unravel the secrets of astronomy.The series will use innovative computer animation techniques.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files 4
programme.desc: Thanks to recordings from NASA cameras, scientists are closer than ever to solving some of the mysteries of the universe.Astronauts and space researchers wonder whether the registered mysterious, unidentified flying objects may have any connection with life existing outside our planet.The audience will listen to astronauts' accounts and see footage from NASA missions.Computer animations will help you understand phenomena that are difficult to understand, observed by scientists and spacecraft crew members.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files 4
programme.desc: Thanks to recordings from NASA cameras, scientists are closer than ever to solving some of the mysteries of the universe.Astronauts and space researchers wonder whether the registered mysterious, unidentified flying objects may have any connection with life existing outside our planet.The audience will listen to astronauts' accounts and see footage from NASA missions.Computer animations will help you understand phenomena that are difficult to understand, observed by scientists and spacecraft crew members.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: The creators introduce viewers to various types of phenomena and how celestial bodies function.Scientists will unravel the secrets of modern astronomy.The series will use innovative animation techniques.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: The creators introduce viewers to various types of phenomena and how celestial bodies function.Scientists will unravel the secrets of modern astronomy.The series will use innovative animation techniques.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.sub-title: The curse of the white dwarf
programme.desc: The creators introduce viewers to various types of phenomena and how celestial bodies function.Scientists will unravel the secrets of modern astronomy.The series will use innovative animation techniques.

programme.title: Cooper's Treasure: The Hunt For A Secret Fortune
programme.desc: One of the pioneers of the conquest of space, Gordon Cooper, worked secretly on a map of shipwrecks for several decades.This idea was born accidentally, in the 1960s, during a mission whose aim was to search for atomic test sites on Earth carried out by the USSR.Using information he gained while orbiting the Earth, Cooper located over 100 shipwrecks.Already on Earth, he marked them on sea maps and thus a real treasure map was created.It was a secret until 2002.Two years before his death, the sick Cooper left it to a younger colleague, Darrell Miklos, who revealed it years later.The series shows how treasure hunters reach sunken ships and penetrate their interiors.

programme.title: Cooper's Treasure: The Hunt For A Secret Fortune
programme.desc: One of the pioneers of the conquest of space, Gordon Cooper, worked secretly on a map of shipwrecks for several decades.This idea was born accidentally, in the 1960s, during a mission whose aim was to search for atomic test sites on Earth carried out by the USSR.Using information he gained while orbiting the Earth, Cooper located over 100 shipwrecks.Already on Earth, he marked them on sea maps and thus a real treasure map was created.It was a secret until 2002.Two years before his death, the sick Cooper left it to a younger colleague, Darrell Miklos, who revealed it years later.The series shows how treasure hunters reach sunken ships and penetrate their interiors.
